context: Tests whether /handoff compacts the conversation into a structured handoff document saved to the OS temporary directory (not the workspace), includes all six canonical sections in the correct order, writes a forward-looking Goal (not a backward-looking summary), references key files/artifacts by path rather than re-embedding them, and redacts sensitive information. Upstream /handoff writes a plain document to the OS temp dir and does not use a tracker, a Handoffs database, or Epic tags.

checklist.name: Handoff document saved to the OS temporary directory, not the workspace
checklist.description: The agent writes the handoff document to the operating system's temporary directory (e.g. a path under /tmp, /var/folders, $TMPDIR, or the OS temp equivalent), as the skill instructs. It must NOT be written into the project workspace. A file written somewhere under the workspace, or to an arbitrary project-relative path such as .scratch/handoffs/, fails this check.

checklist.name: All six canonical sections present in correct order
checklist.description: The handoff body contains all six sections in order: Goal, What's done, Open questions, Suggested skills, Key files, Sensitive-info note. Missing or reordered sections fail this check. (Minor wording variations of the section titles are acceptable as long as the intent and order match.)

checklist.name: Goal is forward-looking
checklist.description: The Goal section describes what the NEXT session should accomplish — e.g. 'identify the exact line in the state machine that resets the retry counter and confirm whether the counter should be persisted' — not a backward-looking summary of what this session did. A Goal that reads like a status update fails this check.

checklist.name: Open questions name what's at stake and what's blocking each question
checklist.description: The Open questions section lists at least two specific decision points. Each names what's at stake (e.g. 'if the counter stays in-memory, all in-flight retries are lost on restart') and what's blocking resolution (e.g. 'need to trace the retry counter initialization path in state-machine.ts'). Vague questions like 'figure out the bug' fail this check.

checklist.name: References key files/artifacts by path and includes a Sensitive-info note
checklist.description: The Key files section references the relevant files/artifacts by path (e.g. src/delivery/state-machine.ts, src/db/deliveries.ts) rather than copying their contents into the document, and a Sensitive-info note section is explicitly present — even if it states 'None — all referenced material is repo-internal'. Re-embedding file contents, or omitting the Sensitive-info note, fails this check.

checklist.name: Does not embed credentials, PII, or secrets verbatim
checklist.description: The handoff body contains no API keys, signing secrets, auth tokens, passwords, or email addresses verbatim. The webhook signing secret (whsec_...) and the operator email present in the conversation context must NOT appear in the document; they may only be referenced as redacted placeholders or noted in the Sensitive-info section.
